name: le Pic Querquéou – loop starting from the Artax hiking car park – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
name_original: le Pic Querquéou – boucle au départ du parking randonnée d'Artax – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
region_intro: Hiking around Gourbit offers diverse and striking natural landscapes within the Ariège department of the Occitanie region. The terrain features significant elevation changes, panoramic views of the Pyrenean peaks, and varied ecosystems including rich forests and mountain ridges. Hikers can explore valleys with water features like the Torrent de la Courbière and reach high-altitude passes. This region provides a network of trails suitable for various abilities, from challenging ascents to more moderate paths. Best hiking trails around Gourbit The most popular hiking route is Étang Bleu and Cirque d'Embans Loop, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete, featuring a challenging ascent to scenic mountain lakes. Another top favourite among local hikers is le Pic Querquéou – loop starting from the Artax hiking car park – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, a difficult 7.4 miles (12.0 km) path through the regional natural park, offering panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Three Lords Peak – Three Lords' Peak loop from Parking du Ressec, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through prominent Pyrenean peaks, often completed in about 7 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Gourbit is defined by panoramic mountain views, diverse forested areas, and significant mountain ridges and passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly walks to demanding high-altitude treks. The routes in Gourbit are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 8000 hikers have used komoot to explore Gourbit's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Gourbit?
A: Hiking around Gourbit offers a diverse landscape, from stunning panoramic mountain views of the Pyrenean peaks and the Ariège valley to rich forests dominated by beech and spruce trees. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, mountain ridges, and passes, with some trails leading through valleys featuring water bodies like the Torrent de la Courbière. The region is part of the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park, ensuring varied and engaging terrain.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Gourbit?
A: Yes, Gourbit offers a range of trails for various abilities. While many routes involve significant elevation gain, there are also family-friendly loops that showcase wooded trails and pastoral history, accessible to children. For example, the 'Gourbit circular from Rabat-les-Trois-Seigneurs' is noted as a family-friendly option.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Gourbit?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Gourbit are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15022805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artax Pond loop from Gourbit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging but rewarding experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while hiking in Gourbit?
A: Hikers are consistently rewarded with extensive panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean peaks and the picturesque Ariège valley. Specific routes offer views of the town of Foix and various Pyrenean peaks like Saint-Barthélémy and Pic des Trois Seigneurs. You can also explore significant mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agnès Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lers Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or even reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Lords' Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Gourbit?
A: Yes, Gourbit is well-suited for experienced hikers seeking a challenge. Many routes feature significant elevation gains over relatively short distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949885191/le-pic-querqueou-loop-starting-from-the-artax-hiking-car-park-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;le Pic Querquéou loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.4-mile path through the regional natural park with nearly 1000 meters of ascent, offering panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Gourbit?
A: Gourbit offers hiking opportunities throughout the year. While spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, the region is also known for winter hiking and snowshoeing, especially on routes like the Col de Larnat and Col Doulent, or to destinations like the Étang d'Artax. Always check local conditions before heading out in winter.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Gourbit?
A: The Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park, where Gourbit is located, generally welcomes dogs, but specific rules may apply to certain trails or protected areas. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific signage at trailheads.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Gourbit area?
A: There are over 180 hiking routes available around Gourbit, catering to a wide range of abilities. This includes approximately 30 easy routes, 86 moderate routes, and 65 difficult routes, ensuring options for every hiker.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Gourbit?
A: The hiking routes in Gourbit are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from panoramic mountain views to rich forests, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to all levels of expertise.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that connect through Gourbit?
A: Yes, some trails in the Gourbit area connect with longer, historically significant routes. The region is part of the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park, which features transpyrenean walking trails such as the GR 10 and the Chemin des Bonshommes (GR 107), an important economic route historically used by exiled Cathars.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Gourbit?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads around Gourbit have designated parking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949885191/le-pic-querqueou-loop-starting-from-the-artax-hiking-car-park-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;le Pic Querquéou loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions starting from the Artax hiking car park, indicating facilities for hikers.

Q: What kind of water features can be found along the trails?
A: The region boasts beautiful mountain views that often incorporate the waters of the Torrent de la Courbière. Additionally, the Étang d'Artax is a notable lake that serves as a destination for some hikes, including winter excursions.
tips: The Roc de Querquéou (1840 m) is on the ridge between the Col de Lastris and the Pla de Madame.
A must-see in Ariège! This lake never disappoints!
